response.justification: Bogdan brings strong staff-level engineering fundamentals and a breadth of experience across full-stack, data integration, and DevOps domains, with current hands-on work on MCP code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure at re:cinq. However, the role demands 8+ years of AI-driven solutions as a hard floor, and his explicit AI/GenAI work is concentrated almost entirely in his most recent position. Python is absent from his skills list despite being a primary requirement, and there is no mention of vector databases, RAG architectures, or LLM evaluation pipelines anywhere in the resume. He is a strong generalist engineer trending into the AI space, but is likely 2–3 years of focused AI delivery work short of what this staff-level, customer-facing GenAI role requires.
strengths: Direct experience building MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure in current role, directly matching a listed key technology
Consistent staff-level seniority across multiple organizations with a track record of mentorship, workshops, and driving engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based dev)
Proven data integration background from high-throughput microservices work at Dunnhumby, handling millions of requests per second with low-latency pipelines
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the listed skills and resume narrative — a critical gap given it is named as a primary language requirement
No mention of vector databases, RAG architectures, or LLM evaluation/observability frameworks, which are core technical competencies for this role
The 8+ years of AI-driven solutions requirement is not met; substantive AI/GenAI work appears limited to the current role started in April 2025
